quick_facts.label: Recruiting route
quick_facts.value: This is a graduate-level Non-Technical Popular Categories (NTPC) post recruited by Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) through centralized employment notices; the relevant recent notice was CEN 05/2024.

quick_facts.label: Education eligibility
quick_facts.value: In CEN 05/2024, the minimum educational qualification for the graduate NTPC posts, including Chief Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor, was a university degree or equivalent. Eligibility must always be checked against the specific new notification.

quick_facts.label: Pay level
quick_facts.value: CEN 05/2024 listed Chief Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor at Level 6 of the 7th CPC Pay Matrix, with initial pay of ₹35,400. This is notification-specific basic-pay context, not a guarantee of current take-home pay.

quick_facts.label: Medical standard
quick_facts.value: The CEN 05/2024 post table specified medical standard B-2 for Chief Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor; candidates must satisfy the applicable Railway medical examination requirements.

quick_facts.label: Recent recruitment context
quick_facts.value: CEN 05/2024 notified 1,736 Chief Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor vacancies across RRBs. The CEN page subsequently posted document-verification and medical-examination scheduling in May 2026, so applicants should use their own RRB's official notices rather than assume a fresh application window is open.

sections.heading: What the role covers
paragraphs: A Chief Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor works in Indian Railways' commercial function. At department level, commercial work spans the sale and marketing of railway transport, passenger-facing service, correct collection, accounting and remittance of traffic receipts, fares and rates, refunds and compensation claims, passenger amenities, ticket checking, and reserved and unreserved ticketing systems. The exact local allocation of supervisory work can vary by railway, division, station, and operational need.

The role's operational knowledge base is broad: a 2025 Railway selection syllabus for Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor includes ticketing and reservation/refund rules, cash remittance and e-balance-sheet processes, ticket-checking procedures and irregular-travel handling, passenger amenities and grievance channels, and relevant goods and parcel commercial rules. This supports a role profile combining frontline passenger-service oversight, revenue/ticketing control, rule compliance, and coordination with commercial staff.
bullets: Supervise or support ticketing, ticket-checking, passenger-service, and commercial-receipt processes within the assigned commercial unit.
Apply published commercial rules on reservations, refunds, concessions, luggage, irregular travel, and related passenger transactions.
Maintain accurate handling, accounting, and remittance of commercial receipts in accordance with Railway procedures.
Respond to passenger-service issues and coordinate with the wider commercial organisation on amenities, complaints, and service delivery.

sections.heading: Entry through RRB NTPC (graduate)
paragraphs: The principal external entry route evidenced by the recent recruitment cycle is RRB NTPC Graduate. CEN 05/2024 listed Chief Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor as a Level-6 graduate post and required a university degree or equivalent. For that notice, the normal age band was 18–33 as of January 1, 2025; a one-time three-year relaxation made the applicable age band 18–36. Age limits, reservations, relaxations, vacancies, fees, and dates can change in later CENs.

The CEN describes a recruitment process involving computer-based tests, document verification, and medical examination. The official CEN 05/2024 page shows that candidates for this cycle progressed to document verification and medical examination in 2026. Candidates should rely only on the RRB and RRB Apply channels named in the live notification.
bullets: Complete the educational, nationality, age, reservation, and medical eligibility conditions in the applicable CEN.
Apply online to one selected RRB when a relevant NTPC Graduate CEN is open.
Prepare for the notified computer-based-test stages; complete document verification and the Railway medical examination if shortlisted.
Do not treat the 2024 vacancy count or its dates as a standing recruitment schedule.

sections.heading: Knowledge and skills to develop
paragraphs: The official CCTS selection syllabus points to the practical capabilities needed after appointment or for advancement in the commercial stream: command of ticketing, reservation, refund, concession, luggage, parcel and freight rules; careful handling of revenue and cash-remittance procedures; ticket-checking and irregular-travel procedures; use of railway digital systems; and awareness of passenger amenities, RailMadad, the 139 helpline, and grievance processes.

Because the commercial department handles both public service and receipt accountability, strong communication, rule interpretation, numerical accuracy, record discipline, customer handling, and calm decision-making in busy passenger environments are especially relevant. These are role-relevant capabilities inferred from the department's stated functions and the published syllabus, rather than separate formal eligibility criteria.
bullets: Ticketing, reservation, fare, refund, concession, luggage, parcel, and ticket-checking rules.
Cash/receipt accountability, remittance procedures, and accurate commercial records.
Passenger communication, complaint handling, and service recovery.
Working knowledge of UTS, mobile ticketing, reservation-related systems, handheld devices, and railway grievance/service applications.
Ability to apply rules consistently while coordinating with ticket-checking and other commercial personnel.

sections.heading: Where and how the work is performed
paragraphs: The post sits in the commercial department of Indian Railways, whose work covers stations and onboard passenger services as well as ticket sales, goods booking, passenger claims, and other commercial activity. Consequently, assignments may be in a railway zone or division and can involve station-based, passenger-service, ticket-checking, or commercial-office work depending on the posting and local organisation.

The work is public-facing and operational rather than limited to a desk: commercial functions include passenger amenities, ticket checking, reserved and unreserved ticketing, claims, fares, and revenue collection. Posting location, rosters, and precise duties are determined by the employing railway administration.
bullets: Potential settings include stations, divisional commercial offices, ticketing/reservation environments, and passenger-service or ticket-checking operations.
The role requires coordination across the commercial organisation and interaction with travelling passengers.
Work patterns and location are posting-dependent; candidates should not assume a particular city, office-only role, or fixed schedule.

sections.heading: Career development context
paragraphs: Career development is within the Railway commercial department, which has responsibilities extending across passenger services, ticketing, claims, fares, freight-related commercial work, and commercial revenue. Published Railway material also shows Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor as a Level-6 selection post and identifies higher commercial levels in departmental restructuring discussions; however, an individual's promotion path depends on applicable Railway Board rules, seniority, eligibility, vacancies, selection processes, and the cadre structure of the particular railway or division.

A sound development path is therefore to build demonstrable proficiency in commercial rules, revenue control, passenger-service systems, and supervisory administration, and to monitor official departmental-selection and personnel notices of the employing railway. Exact promotional designations or timelines should not be assumed from general career websites.
bullets: Develop breadth across passenger, ticketing, ticket-checking, revenue, parcel, and goods-commercial procedures.
Track official personnel and departmental-selection notices in the assigned railway or division.
Promotion opportunities and criteria are cadre- and rule-dependent rather than automatic.

sections.heading: Pay and compensation context
paragraphs: For dated context, CEN 05/2024 placed Chief Commercial cum Ticket Supervisor in Pay Level 6 under the 7th CPC and stated initial pay of ₹35,400. This is the starting basic-pay figure published for that recruitment notice—not an estimate of monthly in-hand pay.

Actual earnings can differ because applicable allowances, deductions, place of posting, and government orders affect pay. Candidates should verify the pay level and all current service/allowance conditions in the CEN and appointment terms applicable to their own recruitment cycle.
bullets: CEN 05/2024 context: 7th CPC Pay Level 6; initial basic pay ₹35,400.
Do not equate initial basic pay with take-home salary.
Check the live official CEN and appointment documentation for the current position.
